records.ety: [F. embche, fr. the verb. See Ambush, v. t.]

defs: A disposition or arrangement of troops for attacking an enemy unexpectedly from a concealed station. Hence: Unseen peril; a device to entrap; a snare.
quotes: Heaven, whose high walls fear no assault or siege Or ambush from the deep.

defs: A concealed station, where troops or enemies lie in wait to attack by surprise.
quotes: Bold in close ambush, base in open field.

defs: The troops posted in a concealed place, for attacking by surprise; liers in wait.
quotes: The ambush arose quickly out of their place.

records.ety: [OE. enbussen, enbushen, OF. embushier, embuissier, F. embcher, embusquer, fr. LL. imboscare; in + LL. boscus, buscus, a wood; akin to G. bush, E. bush. See Ambuscade, Bush.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Ambushed (m"bshd); p. pr. & vb. n. Ambushing.]

defs: To station in ambush with a view to surprise an enemy.
quotes: By ambushed men behind their temple laid, We have the king of Mexico betrayed.

defs: To attack by ambush; to waylay.
To lie in wait, for the purpose of attacking by surprise; to lurk.
quotes: Nor saw the snake that ambushed for his prey.
